item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the nice comment! P. Pigeon appreciates your compliment about his competence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The pigeon being remarkably competent at his job elevates the article above “goofy talking animal”.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: P. Pigeon, Esq. thanks you for your kind words!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: P. Pigeon, Esq. agrees; there can never be too much art of him and his majestic beak.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now THIS is a blessed SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, this was an interesting ride. I definitely want to see more art of this one.

item.title: I see a pigeon, I upvote.
item.content:encoded:
This article is about a pigeon, so of course I'm going to upvote it. +1

In all seriousness though, this is a pretty good SCP, and everyone else here has said everything that can be said for its goodness. Simple, charming, comedic, no real complaints here!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
an interesting sentient pigeon

P. Pigeon, Esq. implores you to look at footnote 3 of the article.

But he does approve of your +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
fun concept along with an interesting sentient pigeon to bring this article together.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fix made, thanks for the comment!

I'm glad you like the article! P. Pigeon gives you an appreciative pigeon noise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this and I just want to draw attention to one thing that I could probably change myself and it's:

It is noted that SCP-4287 is not capable of transporting out of the boardrooms, and must return to its previous location by walking or being carried.

"transporting" should be "teleporting" or something similar :3

item.title: Re: Great
item.content:encoded:
I approve of P. Pigeon Esq and send him a bag of fine birdseed and fruit.

P. Pigeon Esq sends you an embossed thank-you card signed with his footprint custom signature.

I'd have a feeling he'd find it intriguing or if acknowledging that SCP-914 is sapient if he would consider the SCP as a co-worker

Both are definitely possible. The only guarantee is that P. Pigeon Esq would not sit on SCP-914 for extended periods of time.

item.title: Great
item.content:encoded:
I appreciate the entries that maintain a fair tone of levity despite the minority of vocal detractors.

I approve of P. Pigeon Esq and send him a bag of fine birdseed and fruit.

Additionally, I wonder what P. Pigeon Esq thinks of SCP-914.

I'd have a feeling he'd find it intriguing or if acknowledging that SCP-914 is sapient if he would consider the SCP as a co-worker

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: P. Pigeon, Esq. approves of your statement regarding his charm and will withhold comment regarding Foundation filing of his statements and briefs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will defer to the undeniable personal experience and, indeed, charm of P. Pigeon, Esq. in this matter. Therefore, I will consequently also refrain from speculating on the myriad ways in which the Foundation “deals” with the memorandum of P. Pigeon, Esq. and the many oaths and curses they surely do not swear against him whilst doing so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: P. Pigeon, Esq. would like to express his insistence that the Foundation benefits from his carefully crafted memoranda and is not bothered in the slightest

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You had me at “Overly legalistic and bureaucratic pigeon bothering the Foundation with memos”.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: P. Pigeon acknowledges that he is well-versed in humor, but the statement about the beak was well-founded in truth, verily, so he is unsure as to the necessity of the laughter

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Let P. Pigeon know that I might've laughed a little too hard (and am still laughing) at the thought of him pecking that response back to me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: P. Pigeon would like to convey his reassurances that his ego is just the right size proportionally to his beak, which is very large and majestic, thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This dude's ego might be a bit too big for his beak, but you'd be a fool not to respect his business sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: P. Pigeon approves of your response and is grateful for your support

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
:>

coo coo

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was cute and I totally see Zyn’s influence at play here :> +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: P. Pigeon may be available for assistance if you pay his consulting fee in birdseed and fruit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love, love, LOVE this. Now we have two English-speaking birds! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure I catch your meaning here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This pigeon should be a Scientist, not an SCP lol
Plz help me do my paper, Dr. Pigeon

item.title: Re: Slight style change.
item.content:encoded: Huzzah for P. Pigeon's edits displaying nicely across all devices!

item.title: Slight style change.
item.content:encoded:
As Monotype Corsiva hasn't been displaying properly on all devices (as well as not being open-source), I went ahead and swapped it out for Dancing Script from Google Fonts.

It is licensed under the Open Font License.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: P. Pigeon, Esq. graciously accepts your extra +1 and appreciates your statement about the bird bath.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All fair points. I would imagine P. Pigeon, Esq. would have some sort of bird bath installed. An extra +1 for the phrase "pigeon monologue".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel personally attacked.

P. Pigeon responds
birb do a headtilt

is this the first time it's been mentioned that the Foundation has interns?

I want to say probably not, but I'm not sure if there were any named interns mentioned before.

Regardless, would definitely be interested in seeing some followup on what the hell it means to be an intern for a secret organization - maybe a tale?

Hmm… a thought! I've written some slice-of-life stuff about researchers handling non-SCP lowkey anomalies. Kiryu Labs could use some interns…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Does nobody know the difference between 'sentient' and 'sapient' anymore?

I feel personally attacked.

+1.

(Also, is this the first time it's been mentioned that the Foundation has interns? Wasn't able to find anything else with a quick search. Regardless, would definitely be interested in seeing some followup on what the hell it means to be an intern for a secret organization - maybe a tale?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't see how that ties into the fact that you have sapient bird that can write documents by pecking. Yeah, both things involve paper, but I don't see how one leads to the other.

The bird is a caricature of the Foundation, as perceived by Serpent's Hand pranksters. It does caricature Foundation things.

Also, site image shows the pigeon sitting in a shallow pool of water but it's labeled a "board room".

Yeah, I was browsing wikimedia commons and I thought that image looked funny. It's admittedly not a perfect fit, but I found it so charming and otherwise fitting that I really wanted to use it (plus with the image shrunk down, it's not super obvious there's water involved?).

What kinds of boardrooms have large, shallow puddles?

My headcanon is that P. Pigeon, Esq. felt that he needed a water feature installed so he can keep himself well-groomed and out of the sinks that he might slip and fall into and injure himself. Furthermore, given his delicate condition with regards to sitting in one place for long amounts of time, surely it makes sense to have… [insert pigeon monologue here]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 because I like birds. +1 because this bird has a law degree.+1 for the bird switching between a formal, British "on the record" accent and a gruff Brooklyn accent when not being interviewed. +1 for the bird requiring a team of interns to move its lazy butt around.

-1 because the paper eating and paper poop thing seems like a bit of an odd addition. I don't see how that ties into the fact that you have sapient bird that can write documents by pecking. Yeah, both things involve paper, but I don't see how one leads to the other. That said, I've owned birds, and I wished they pooped paper (and were smart enough to aim for a receptacle).

Also, site image shows the pigeon sitting in a shallow pool of water but it's labeled a "board room". What kinds of boardrooms have large, shallow puddles? Not a nitpick, just a thing I noticed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dang, now I can't unhear that voice as the pigeon!

Thanks for the +1 and the earworm now. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, I wrote this with Stewie's voice somewhat in mind. :)

Thanks for the upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reasons I read the bird's text in Stewie from Family Guy's voice. Upvote for that image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this is their variant of the "You know who's dumb? You! This is what you look like! That's how dumb you look!" taunt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see the Serpent's Upraised Middle Finger has gotten creative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: P. Pigeon, Esq. thanks you for your kind words and approves of your birb upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Listen, I'm a simple woman. I see a bird, I upvote.

This is positively charming and the image of this pigeon pecking away at a keyboard has me grinning at mine.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I don't really buy that it satisfies the "An item that is very good at doing the thing its supposed to do, too good in fact.

It's a debatable aspect. P. Pigeon, Esq. can write articulate, professional 20-page briefs by pecking at a stack of paper. Not that the Foundation necessarily wants him to, but it is a thing he can do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Business pigeon thanks you for the +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love it!

Yay! I'm glad you enjoyed the read!

I know from experience that Zyn is a pleasure to co-author with

Awweh~ :3c

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thankee, Ooga! You're right, Zyn was an absolute blast to write with!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it! I know from experience that Zyn is a pleasure to co-author with, and Rev Fox, you brought some excellent dialogue to the table.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was looking through on my phone, and the whole text was like, twice it's size.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm… are you viewing this on a mobile device? What browser? It looks fine on most devices I think, but I'll double check with the tech team to see if we can fix the difference in displays.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bit of a nitpick but the font for this on the mainlist is rather too big for my taste and pretty distracting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Business pigeon is cute
+1 because pigeon

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Nice work, I'm glad somebody tackled the "roam the site" cliche. Helpful but has personality. +1

That said, I don't really buy that it satisfies the "An item that is very good at doing the thing its supposed to do, too good in fact." cliche.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the nice comment!

A competent, smart birb

That's a big part of the premise! Rev picked a pigeon because of the slang/colloquial term referring to someone being excessively nitpicky, but then we thought, "but what if the pigeon was actually better organized than the Foundation.

We appreciate your upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A competent, smart birb with british accent? That's exactly the type of light-hearted article I needed after reading a bunch of horrific ones. Upvoted.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Big thanks to everyone in chat and forums who read this over before the mainlisting! ETA: and of course to my wonderful co-author!

Image is from Wikimedia Commons: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Columba_livia_-_02.jpg and licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-Share Alike 4.0 International license.

This has been in the works for awhile…IRL got in the way frequently. I did have a lot of fun in the brainstorming stage with this, and Rev Fox was super great with handling all the formatting and the funny dialogue.

I hope you enjoy the read!

also this is totally irrelevant but I think seeing birds sitting down in random places is absolutely hilarious idk why

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
ClicheCon 2019 entry! A big thanks to Zyn for collaborating on co-authoring this!

++ Heaps of SCP-4287-1 go to ... ++
-- Seriously, though... y'all are awesome! --

For Forum Crits, I'd like to thank RockTeethMothEyes and SoullessSingularity!

For Chat Crits, rattles, XilasCrowe, and Weryllium knocked it out of the field!

Thank you all!

++ Info Panel ++
-- Info Panel --

P. Pigeon, Esq.

ClicheCon Collab. Entry
Category 3
By:
Reverend Fox
Zyn


Cliches:
  • Anomaly Cliche: An anomaly so helpful/harmless, the Foundation lets it roam the Site and has minimal containment procedures. Being helpful/harmless is optional.
  • Secondary Anomaly Cliche: An item that is very good at doing the thing its supposed to do, too good in fact.
  • Story Element Cliche: An interview in which the anomaly is overly cryptic, followed by the interviewer suffering.
